technique_name: view change log merging - tighter anchor via checkpoint/backup MAX
claim: Replacing the either/or anchor selection (backup XOR checkpoint) with MAX(backup_anchor, checkpoint_seq) strictly increases or preserves the recovery anchor in every reachable state, reducing the archiver contiguity gap that MaxContiguousFrom must bridge. Concretely: in any state where ce_backup_alive=TRUE, ce_backup_applied /= {}, and ce_checkpoint_seq > MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied), the current spec sets anchor = MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied), losing checkpoint-guaranteed durability. The proposed spec sets anchor = MAX(MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied), ce_checkpoint_seq), recovering at least as many effects without archiver dependency.
preconditions: CEViewChange uses an anchor computation that selects between backup max-applied and checkpoint seq but never combines them. The checkpoint guarantees all effects up to ce_checkpoint_seq are durable (checkpointed to L1 or equivalent). The backup guarantees all effects up to MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied) are replicated. These two durability guarantees are independent and composable: the safe anchor is their maximum.
structural_delta: Recovery anchor precision: BEFORE anchor is min(backup_max, checkpoint_seq) in the case where backup is alive but lagging behind checkpoint. AFTER anchor is max(backup_max, checkpoint_seq) always. This shrinks the archiver contiguity window (the range [anchor+1, ce_seq-1] that must be recovered from archivers) by up to (checkpoint_seq - backup_max) slots when checkpoint leads backup.
diff:
--- a/spec.tla
+++ b/spec.tla
@@ CEViewChange anchor computation
 CEViewChange ==
     /\ ~ce_alive
     /\ LET new_ts == ce_authority_ts + 1
            new_session == ce_primary + 1
-           anchor == IF ce_backup_alive /\ ce_backup_applied /= {}
-                     THEN MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied)
-                     ELSE ce_checkpoint_seq
+           backup_anchor == IF ce_backup_alive /\ ce_backup_applied /= {}
+                            THEN MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied)
+                            ELSE 0
+           anchor == IF backup_anchor >= ce_checkpoint_seq
+                     THEN backup_anchor
+                     ELSE ce_checkpoint_seq
            available == CEArchiverSeqsForSession(ce_primary)
            recovered == MaxContiguousFrom(anchor + 1, available)
        IN /\ ce_alive' = TRUE
rationale: The current anchor computation treats backup liveness and checkpoint as mutually exclusive durability sources. But they are independent: checkpoint guarantees durability via L1/external truth, backup guarantees durability via hot replication. When backup is alive but has fallen behind the checkpoint (e.g., backup applied up to seq 3, but checkpoint covers up to seq 5), the current code anchors at 3 and must recover seqs 4-5 from archivers, even though they are already guaranteed durable by the checkpoint. Taking the MAX of both sources is always safe because: (1) checkpoint_seq <= ce_seq - 1 by CECheckpoint guard, so it never exceeds what was produced; (2) MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied) <= ce_seq - 1 by P9/P12; (3) both represent contiguous prefixes of the log. The improvement is monotone: anchor' >= anchor in every state, so recovered' >= recovered, preserving P4 (SurgicalRollbackPrecision) since a higher recovered value only retains more effects from the old session, and the new session still starts from recovered+1.
